title: space_join guard: match on (Option, Option) before network call is correct and complete
description: The match (input.invite_code, input.space_id) { (None,None) => Err, (Some,Some) => Err, _ => {} } fires before session_parts and before any network call. This is the correct placement — it avoids the session lookup allocation and any HTTP overhead on bad input. The match is exhaustive at the type level (the _ arm covers both valid cases), which means adding a third field to SpaceJoinInput in the future would NOT silently bypass the guard — the wildcard still correctly allows the one-Some cases. A future contributor might suggest replacing this with an if/else chain; resist it, the match makes the XOR structure explicit and compiler-visible.
